# devin_secret (Resource)

Manages an org-level secret made available to Devin sessions. The Devin API has no secret update endpoint, so any change forces a replacement. The secret value is never returned by the API and cannot be imported.

## Example Usage

```terraform
variable "npm_token" {
  description = "npm registry token for frontend builds"
  type        = string
  sensitive   = true
}

# Exposed to Devin sessions as the NPM_TOKEN environment variable.
resource "devin_secret" "npm_token" {
  org_id = devin_organization.frontend.org_id
  key    = "NPM_TOKEN"
  value  = var.npm_token
  note   = "Read-only npm token for CI builds"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `key` (String) Secret name, exposed to sessions as an environment variable. Must follow environment variable naming conventions.
- `org_id` (String) Organization ID that owns this secret.
- `value` (String, Sensitive) Secret value. Stored encrypted by Devin and never returned by the API.

### Optional

- `is_sensitive` (Boolean) Whether the value is hidden in the Devin UI. Defaults to true.
- `note` (String) Human-readable note describing the secret.
- `type` (String) Secret type: key-value, cookie, or totp. Defaults to key-value.

### Read-Only

- `secret_id` (String) Secret ID (assigned by Devin).
